name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise workflow automation platform, providing IT service management, customer service, HR, and security operations through a single cloud-based platform. Under CEO Bill McDermott, the company has tripled revenues since 2019 and achieved consistent 20%+ subscription revenue growth.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: ServiceNow has embedded AI across its entire workflow platform through Now Assist and generative AI capabilities, with strategic acquisitions of Element AI, Hitch Works, Era Software, and Moveworks — earning 2 points for AI deployment with measurable results [1]. McDermott states ServiceNow has 'eliminated 90% of customer service use cases that once required human workers,' demonstrating transformative impact. Innovation track record includes tripling revenue since 2019 and 244 transactions over $1M in Q4 (40% YoY growth), earning 3 points [2]. Market share leadership in ITSM is dominant (2 points). R&D intensity is above industry average with continuous platform expansion (1 point). Pipeline includes Moveworks integration adding 100bps to growth.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: ServiceNow's aspiration to be 'the AI Defining Enterprise Software Company of the 21st Century' is specific and ambitious (mission floor 2) [2]. Named to Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For, Forbes Most Trusted Companies, and Fortune World's Best Workplaces, suggesting strong retention (2 points). McDermott's 'people before profits' philosophy and 'hungry and humble' culture are distinctive. No significant community/charitable program data found (0 points). No insider buying evidence (0 points). McDermott's personal brand drives culture more than institutional mission.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: ServiceNow scores nearly perfectly across all Growth/Prospect metrics: D/E of 18.5% (1.0), ROE of 15.5% (1.0 at 2x weight), revenue growth of 20.7% YoY (1.0), 5yr CAGR ~25% (1.0 at 2x weight), forward P/E of 22.1 (0.95), profit margin of 13.2% (0.66), and PEG of 1.06 (0.97 at 2x weight) [1]. The only below-target metrics are forward P/E slightly above 20 and profit margin below the 20% threshold, but exceptional growth metrics compensate at elevated weights.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: ServiceNow IS the AI-enabled workflow platform — the company is building AI, not being disrupted by it. McDermott states that 'AI is eating features and functions companies' while ServiceNow displaces them, making it a net beneficiary of AI disruption [2]. The platform has achieved 98% renewal rates and 20%+ subscription growth, with AI (Now Assist) driving larger deal sizes. The company has eliminated 90% of customer service use cases through AI, demonstrating how AI enhances rather than threatens the platform. Meaningful AI revenue through Now Assist and agentic AI capabilities earns the opportunity point.

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow under McDermott shows strong elements of infinite game thinking — consistent investment in platform capabilities, acquisitions for long-term positioning, and a culture that balances growth with employee wellbeing. However, McDermott's stark warning that AI could push new grad unemployment to 30% reveals a finite mindset about labor, and the intense rivalry with Salesforce is framed competitively.

just_cause.analysis: ServiceNow's ambition to be 'the AI Defining Enterprise Software Company of the 21st Century' is bold and forward-looking, but it's more about market positioning than societal benefit. The cause has been consistent under McDermott but hasn't survived a leadership transition test (2 points) [2].

trusting_teams.analysis: Named to Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For and Fortune World's Best Workplaces. McDermott united 29,000+ employees under one vision. The 'hungry and humble' culture is distinctive and frequently cited. Evidence of high trust and empowerment (2 points) [2].

worthy_rivals.analysis: The competitive language against Salesforce is framed as 'devouring' rivals — McDermott says AI will 'obliterate software vendors doing busywork.' This is finite competitive language focused on defeating rather than learning (0 points) [2].

existential_flexibility.analysis: ServiceNow has expanded from ITSM into HR, customer service, security, and now agentic AI — representing meaningful but evolutionary platform extension rather than transformative reinvention (1 point).

courage_to_lead.analysis: The Moveworks acquisition and $5B share buyback authorization demonstrate confidence in the AI strategy. McDermott's willingness to cannibalize existing workflows with AI automation shows courage, though the company's growth reduces the perceived risk of these bets (1 point) [1].

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the fastest-growing enterprise software company in history with 20%+ organic subscription revenue growth, a 98% renewal rate, and accelerating AI-driven deal sizes — 244 transactions over $1M in Q4 representing 40% YoY growth [1]. The 2026 guidance of $15.5-15.6B subscription revenue (19.5-20% growth) with 32% operating margins and 36% FCF margins positions ServiceNow as a rare combination of growth and profitability. The Moveworks acquisition adds 100bps to growth, and the $5B buyback authorization signals management confidence. At a PEG of 1.06, the stock is reasonably valued for a company generating $2B+ in quarterly free cash flow.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on valuation compression risk — at a trailing P/E of 66x, any deceleration in growth could trigger multiple contraction. McDermott's warning that AI could push new grad unemployment to 30% reflects a broader risk: if enterprises use ServiceNow to eliminate jobs rather than create new workflows, regulatory or political backlash could constrain growth [2]. The intense battle with Salesforce for agentic AI dominance creates execution risk, and the company's 13.2% profit margin lags peers, suggesting heavy reinvestment requirements. The stock has already declined 6.4% since last earnings, and forward P/E of 22 sits above the IOWN 20 target.
key_catalysts: Moveworks acquisition integration adding ~100bps to subscription revenue growth from Q1 2026
Agentic AI capabilities driving larger enterprise deal sizes — 244 transactions over $1M in Q4, up 40% YoY
Operating margin expansion to 32% (up 100bps) and FCF margin to 36% in 2026, demonstrating operating leverage
key_risks: Trailing P/E of 66x creates significant multiple compression risk if subscription growth decelerates below 20%
AI-driven labor displacement warnings from CEO McDermott could trigger regulatory backlash constraining enterprise software adoption
Intense competitive battle with Salesforce in agentic AI and ITSM could require accelerated investment, pressuring margins
sources: Cloud Wars — AI and ServiceNow Devouring Many SaaS Vendors, Says CEO Bill McDermott
Fortune — ServiceNow CEO says new grad unemployment could reach 30%, March 2026
Seeking Alpha — ServiceNow projects 20% subscription revenue growth for 2026
Sustainalytics — ServiceNow ESG Risk Rating: Strong
